summary: Comprehensive testing of the new 'Forgot Password' feature in ReversePicks. All backend and frontend tests passed. The feature allows users to reset their password after re-verifying their Whop membership.

issues: Badge shows 'Verified Access Re-Verified' instead of 'Lifetime Access Re-Verified' - the access_type is being set to 'Verified' as fallback instead of actual access type from API response

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with valid member email returns verified=true with session token
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with non-member email returns 401 'No active subscription found'
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with member email but no account returns 404 'No account found'
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with short password (<6 chars) returns 400 error
Backend: Login with new password after reset works correctly
Frontend: Forgot Password link visible on password login step
Frontend: Clicking Forgot Password shows reset form with 'Access Re-Verified' badge
Frontend: Password mismatch validation shows 'Passwords do not match' error
Frontend: Short password validation - button disabled when password < 6 chars (correct behavior)
Frontend: Back to Login button returns to password form
Frontend: Valid password reset auto-logs in and shows main app
Frontend: Login with new password after reset works correctly
Frontend: Owner auto-login (josselj001@gmail.com) still works - bypasses password
Frontend: Existing login flow with correct password still works
Frontend: Wrong password shows 'Invalid password' error
action_items: Minor: In startForgotPassword function (line 338), the accessType is set to 'Verified' as fallback. Consider using the actual access_type from the API response for better UX (e.g., 'Lifetime Access Re-Verified' instead of 'Verified Access Re-Verified')
critical_code_review_comments: The forgot password flow correctly re-verifies Whop membership before allowing reset
The reset-password endpoint properly checks for existing account before allowing reset
Password validation (min 6 chars) is enforced both frontend and backend
Auto-login after reset works correctly by storing session token in localStorage
seed_data_creation: Password for rijulgauchan1@gmail.com was reset to 'resettest1' during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Forgot Password feature fully tested and working. The flow: email verification → password form → click 'Forgot Password?' → re-verify membership → reset form → enter new password → auto-login. rijulgauchan1@gmail.com now has password 'resettest1'. All previous auth tests from iteration_6 still pass.

feature_verification.feature_description: Allows users to reset their password by re-verifying their Whop membership
key_flows_tested: Forgot Password link visibility on password step
Reset form display with membership re-verification badge
